response.justification: The candidate has a broad software development background with genuine strengths in distributed systems and testing culture, but falls critically short of the role's core requirements. The position mandates 8 years of C++ experience, yet the resume shows only a brief ~6-month stint at National Instruments writing C/C++ components and a single C++ bachelor thesis project — likely under 1 year total. The candidate's primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, and there is no evidence of cloud platform engineering, infrastructure architecture at scale, or technical leadership of cloud or DevOps teams. A current career gap compounds the concern.
strengths: Demonstrated testing culture and TDD advocacy spanning over a decade, including workshops, open-source test tooling, and consistent application across roles
Real exposure to high-throughput distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby), showing some relevant infrastructure instinct
Consistent staff-level and leadership contributions including mentoring, DevOps setup, and community knowledge-sharing
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project) versus the 8-year minimum requirement — a hard disqualifier
No cloud platform engineering experience at production scale; AWS CDK usage at Optilyz and a personal Kubernetes project do not constitute cloud architecture leadership
No evidence of technical leadership over cloud or infrastructure teams, which is the core function of this role
